{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this burial mound was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m

Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|Artifacts recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|burial goods recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m

An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m

The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|burial goods recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/gakoen/yamatechugakkoku/ryohokushogakko/koukusyoukai/1554536099611.html Kakogawa city Board of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakogawa, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:Saijō Kofun Cluster]]

The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|burial goods recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/gakoen/yamatechugakkoku/ryohokushogakko/koukusyoukai/1554536099611.html Kakogawa city Board of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]]{{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakogawa, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]]}} [[en:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|burial goods recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/gakoen/yamatechugakkoku/ryohokushogakko/koukusyoukai/1554536099611.html Kakogawa city Board of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakogawa, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:Saijō Kofun Cluster]]

The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|burial goods recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/gakoen/yamatechugakkoku/ryohokushogakko/koukusyoukai/1554536099611.html Kakogawa city Board of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakogawa, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:Saijō Kofun Cluster]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|burial goods recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/gakoen/yamatechugakkoku/ryohokushogakko/koukusyoukai/1554536099611.html Kakogawa city Board of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakogawa, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:aijō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn: failed]]

height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|burial goods recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/gakoen/yamatechugakkoku/ryohokushogakko/koukusyoukai/1554536099611.html Kakogawa city Board of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakogawa, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]]}} [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:aijō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kakogawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Saijō Kofun cluster | native_name =西条古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gyōjazuka Kofun within the Saijō Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Saijō Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Saijō Kofun cluster'''|西条古墳群||}} is a group of three ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Yamate neighborhood of the city of [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1973.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172615|title=西条古墳群|trans-title=Saijō Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Saijō Kofun cluster is located on a long terrace extending north from the Innanno Plateau on the east bank of the [[Kako River|Kakogawa River]], about ten kilometers from the river's mouth. The cluster consisted of dozens of tumuli, but most were destroyed due to the development of residential land, and now only three remain. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1995-1996 The {{nihongo|{{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|行者塚古墳||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the southwest and has an overall length of around 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa City and one of the largest in the [[Harima Province|Harima region]]. On the surface of the mound, in addition to ''[[fukiishi]]'', a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen. In addition, two extensions on the left and right of the mound constriction and two locations on the rear circle exist, which are believed to have had a ceremonial purpose, as representational ''haniwa'' have been found. The tumulus's surrounded by a double moat. The [[Burial chamber|burial facilities]] in the posterior circular portion consist of three clay burials, which have not been investigated. Two more burials were found in the anterior portion, which included [[grave goods]] such as gold and copper engraved metal fittings, an iron ax, tubular bronze products, horse harness, and iron swords. It is estimated that this Kofun was built in the middle of the Kofun period, around the end of the 4th century to early 5th century. The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
posterior circular portion: 68 m dia
anterior width:55m||late 4 - early 5th C |- |'''Hitozuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|47|0.70|N|134|53|10.05|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 61.5m
height10.4m||early 5th C |- |'''Amazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|53.75|N|134|53|11.90|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||diameter45m
height 6m||mid-5th C |} ==Gallery== File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun File:Gyojazuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-nishi-4.jpg|Gyōjazuka Kofun, ceremonial platform File:Hitozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Hitozuka Kofun File:Amatsuka Kofun (Kakogawa), funkyu.jpg|Amazuka Kofun 行者塚古墳出土 巴形銅器.JPG|burial goods recovered from the Gyōjazuka Kofun ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Saijo Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/gakoen/yamatechugakkoku/ryohokushogakko/koukusyoukai/1554536099611.html Kakogawa city Board of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kakogawa, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]]}} [[sn:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Saijō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:aijō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn: failed]]

The horse harness is the oldest to have been discovered in the [[Kinai region]] of Japan, and the style of the grave goods indicates a strong cultural connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]] and mainland Asia.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The {{nihongo|'''Hitozuka Kofun'''|人塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|'''Amazuka Kofun'''|尼塚古墳||}} are both large circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mounds, surrounded yay moats. Neither has been excavated, but they are believed to date from the 5th century. The site is open to the public as an [[archaeological park]], and is located about a 15-minute walk from [[Kanno Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kakogawa Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Saijō Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size !Date |- |'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''||{{coords|34|46|54.95|N|134|53|18.10|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 99m
